name: InVision Design Platform API
what_it_does: Design collaboration platform with REST API — providing prototyping, design feedback collection (InVision Prototype), real-time design collaboration whiteboard (InVision Freehand), and design system management (InVision DSM), for design teams to create, share, and handoff UI designs, now primarily focusing on Freehand.
use_cases: Managing InVision prototypes and collecting stakeholder feedback via REST API
Accessing InVision Freehand whiteboard data for team collaboration tracking
Integrating InVision with project management tools via API for design-to-development workflow
Querying InVision prototype comment and feedback data for UX research documentation
Managing InVision team members and project access via API
not_for: New prototyping projects (InVision Prototype being sunset — use Figma for new work)
Developer handoff specs at scale (InVision Inspect sunsetting — use Zeplin or Figma)
Enterprise design system tokens (use Figma Tokens or Supernova for modern design systems)
best_when: A design team with existing InVision Freehand workflows needs API access for project management and collaboration data — particularly for whiteboard and brainstorming workflows.
avoid_when: You're starting fresh with prototyping and design handoff (Figma has largely replaced InVision's core prototyping use case); existing InVision Prototype subscriptions are being sunset.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code flow. API key for service-to-service access. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. Webhook for prototype comment events and project events. Freehand API separate from Prototype API. Sketch plugin for import. No fine-grained scopes. InVision undergoing strategic pivot to Freehand.

pricing.notes: New York company. Founded 2011. Raised $350M+ total. Backed by Accel, ICONIQ, Tiger Global, Spark Capital. Once valued at $1.9B (2020). Significant market share loss to Figma — InVision Prototype and Design being sunset. Pivoting to InVision Freehand (collaborative whiteboard) as core product. Layoffs through 2023. 6M+ designers used InVision at peak. Clark Valberg and Ben Nadel founders. Important platform risk: core prototyping products being discontinued.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per account; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for prototypes, comments, and project management. OAuth2 auth. Documentation increasingly outdated as products sunset. Significant platform risk — InVision Prototype being sunset in 2024. Freehand has separate, evolving API. API for migrating away from InVision may be more relevant than building new integrations.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Project reads idempotent. Comment reads idempotent. Screen reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: MAJOR: InVision Prototype and Design products being sunset — do not start new integrations
Freehand is the remaining core product — build only Freehand integrations going forward
InVision Inspect (developer handoff) also being sunset — migrate to Figma or Zeplin
Documentation is increasingly stale as company restructures — verify API still active
Export and migration from InVision should be prioritized over new integrations

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. US data residency. OAuth2 auth. Platform sunset risk — export IP/design data before InVision decommissions services. Design data contains unreleased product IP — prioritize data export. Significant organizational instability (layoffs, pivots) creates ongoing vendor risk.
